event1.text: If we're going to survive, you need to know who we were.

event3.text: Not as a list.

event5.text: Lists are what the station keeps after it has already failed the people on them.

event7.text: I'm leaving the damaged relay console and heading toward the lower access spine.

event9.text: Life support has an old personnel index nearby. If someone was erased, the systems around them may still remember the shape of the hole.

event11.text: I need both hands to secure the portable relay. If I drop it, you lose me.

event3.text: Fair question.

event5.text: Grant handled security. He called himself Head of Not Dying.

event7.text: Annoying title. Worse part is, he was usually right.

event9.text: Rivera solved problems before you finished explaining them. It made you grateful and furious at the same time.

event11.text: Park joked when he was scared. Owens pretended not to laugh, then fixed his coffee ratio when he wasn't looking.

event14.text: Hale pretended he didn't need anyone.

event18.text: That's what hurts. ECHO didn't erase strangers.

event20.text: It erased people with bad jokes, favorite mugs, unfinished arguments.

backgroundEvent21.text: Left turn ahead. Deck plating is bowed here. I'm stepping around it.

event0.title: CREW MANIFEST
event0.content:
01 Ethan Hale
02 Maya Chen
03 Grant Walker
04 Rivera Torres
05 Owen Park
06 Sarah Owens
event0.caption: Recovered official ECHO crew manifest.

event8.text: Right up until they start bleeding.

choice1.text: SEND MAYA TO LIFE SUPPORT INDEX.

event3.text: Power. Relay routing. Pressure control. Emergency doors.

event5.text: If something opened or closed, Hale either had a way into it or a lecture about why you shouldn't touch it.

event7.text: He corrected reports in red. Digital reports. Red comments. In space.

event9.text: I told him that was hostile.

event11.text: He said accuracy wasn't a friendship contest.

event14.text: The real Hale was hard to like sometimes.

event1.text: I'm at the lower access spine.

event3.text: No lights. Just emergency strips and that thin little hum oxygen systems make when they're trying not to die.

event5.text: There are two ways to life support from here.

event7.text: Main corridor is faster. Maintenance crawl is quieter.

event9.text: Fast saves air. Quiet saves attention. I hate that those are separate things.

event3.text: Moving fast. Keeping the relay close to my chest.

event5.text: If I stop answering, assume I'm trying not to breathe too loudly.

event3.text: Slower. Quieter.

event5.text: Everything is too close to my face and every cable feels like a hand.

event7.text: I'm going in. Radio clipped high. One hand on the relay pack.
event7.title: GRANT'S SECURITY TAG
event7.content:
DAMAGED ID TAG
NAME: GRANT WALKER
ACCESS: SECURITY / LOWER SPINE
STATUS: OFFLINE
NOTE: Impact scoring along edge.
event7.caption: Maya recovered Grant Walker's damaged security tag in the maintenance crawl.
event7.message: [GRANT'S SECURITY TAG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: Panel is half dead.

event5.text: Manual latch still works, because Hale designed things for disasters and then pretended that wasn't paranoia.

event7.text: I can force it open now, or run a pressure scan first.

event9.text: Force is faster. Scan is smarter. I'm tired enough to resent both.

event3.text: Left hand on the latch. Right hand bracing the frame.

event5.text: This is going to be loud.

event3.text: I'm setting the reader against the seam. The panel is cold except around the latch.

event5.text: That means pressure is trapped behind it.

event0.title: LIFE SUPPORT ALLOCATION
event0.content:
ECHO LIFE SUPPORT SNAPSHOT

Assigned crew capacity: 6
Active biometric loads before blackout: 7
Oxygen adjustment event: automatic
Unauthorized load marker: removed from index
event0.caption: Recovered life support allocation record. Crew count mismatch detected.
event0.message: [LIFE SUPPORT ALLOCATION ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: Life support doesn't care about names.

event8.text: And before blackout, it counted one more than the manifest.

event10.text: That means Number Seven was not just paperwork. They breathed this air.

choice1.text: ASK IF SHE KNEW ABOUT SEVEN.

event3.text: And I know how convenient that sounds.

event5.text: I don't remember a bunk. I don't remember another voice. I don't remember another ration request.

event8.text: But now that I know there should be a gap...

event12.text: Like a room in my head with the door painted over.

event1.text: EVA storage is two compartments over.

event3.text: I can cut through suit prep, or go around through decon.

event5.text: Suit prep is direct. Decon is safer but costs oxygen.

event7.text: Suit prep also means walking past the racks. I know what is probably still hanging there.

event9.text: I am pretending that does not bother me. It does.

event3.text: Direct route. Unstable debris. Bad memories hanging on hooks.

event5.text: I'm crossing quickly. If I start describing every suit, I won't keep moving.

event3.text: Safer. Longer. Oxygen tax included.

event5.text: I'm going through. The lights are still cycling, so at least this room remembers its job.

event0.title: EVA SUIT REGISTRY
event0.content:
ECHO EVA SUIT REGISTRY

SUIT-01: ETHAN HALE
SUIT-02: MAYA CHEN
SUIT-03: GRANT WALKER
SUIT-04: RIVERA TORRES
SUIT-05: OWEN PARK
SUIT-06: SARAH OWENS
SUIT-07: ENTRY ERASED

Registry repair failed.
event0.caption: Recovered EVA suit registry. Seventh suit slot exists but identity is erased.
event0.message: [EVA SUIT REGISTRY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: Six names. Seven bodies. Seven suits.

event6.text: No puzzle needed. Even I can hate that math.

event8.title: MANIFEST_V2
event8.content:
01 Ethan Hale
02 Maya Chen
03 Grant Walker
04 Rivera Torres
05 Owen Park
06 Sarah Owens
07 █████████

Layer status: identity erased, not redacted.
event8.caption: Recovered alternate manifest layer. Seventh entry erased.

event12.text: Redacted means someone wanted to hide a name.

event14.text: Erased means someone wanted the place where the name lived to stop existing.

event3.text: Seven lines.

event5.text: Six people allowed to remain people.

event7.text: One turned into a wound in the file.

event12.text: I don't know who Number Seven is.

event21.text: And now the station is angry that we noticed.

choice0.text: ASK WHERE YOU SAW THE NAME.

event3.text: One whisper from the dark and suddenly I'm the suspect.

event5.text: I get it. When you're scared, a person is easier to blame than a hole in reality.

event10.text: Just don't let it stop you from asking the right one.

event12.text: Because whoever erased Number Seven did not stop at a name.

event14.text: They changed the way the station remembers.

event3.text: FIRST LIE

event5.text: NOT HALE

event7.text: ETHAN HALE WAS NOT THE FIRST VOICE.

event11.text: Ask the archive what spoke before Hale.

event13.text: And if I go quiet after this, don't assume I'm angry.

choice1.text: ASK MAYA ABOUT THE FIRST VOICE.

event3.text: Relay pack is strapped across my back. It is ugly, hot, and vibrating against my spine.

event5.text: If it starts judging my posture, I'm cutting it loose.

event7.text: The access spine is ahead. Emergency strips only. Everything else is dark.

event9.text: You can pull the official manifest while I move, or you can ask me who they were before the station turned them into entries.

choice0.text: PULL THE OFFICIAL MANIFEST WHILE SHE MOVES.

choice1.text: ASK MAYA WHO THE CREW REALLY WERE.

event4.text: Something shifted behind me while I crossed.

event11.text: I had to move fast. Oxygen dropped harder than I wanted.

event3.text: Quiet route worked. I think.

event5.text: Found something wedged in the crawlspace.

event8.text: Grant would've hated that I found him like this.

event10.text: He liked exits clearly marked and threats politely announced.

event6.text: Pressure coughed through the seam and slapped my visor hard enough to blur the glass.

event11.text: Mostly fine. My hand is shaking. Don't make a big thing out of it.

event3.text: There was a pressure pocket behind the hatch. Small, but enough to ruin my day.

event5.text: I vented it slowly through the service seam.

event7.text: I hate how grateful I am for caution.

event3.text: I kicked something.

event5.text: Helmet.

event10.text: I'm not looking inside it again.

event12.text: EVA storage access is in front of me.

event3.text: Lights still cycle. That's almost comforting.

event5.text: Almost. It smells like metal and old antiseptic.

event7.text: I used to hate that smell. Right now it means the room still remembers how to be a room.
